structured.title: Parent and Child Discuss Pocket Money for Football
structured.overview: In this informal family conversation, a parent and child (likely Elliot) review how much pocket money the child has left. The parent notes that some money has already been spent, possibly on toys or sweets, and that the child has more cash than is reasonable to take to football. They decide the child will only take £5, not the full amount or their whole wallet, and the parent reminds the child to be polite and not to spend all of it. They plan to ask the mother later about what happened to the rest of the money. At the end, they prepare to leave and the parent checks a device or app that had not been working previously.

apps_results.content:
## Money discussion  
- Child expected about £60 saved  
- Real amount reduced by £30 on “bowlies”  
- Parent suggests asking mom about missing money  

## Pocket money for outing  
- Child tries to take £30 out  
- Parent limits to £5, keeps remaining cash  
- Wallet stays at home, only small amount allowed  

## Spending rules  
- Parent warns not to spend all £5  
- Reminds child to be polite and “kind to daddy”  
- Notes child already has “a lot of money” for age  

## Tech / SOS Super issue  
- Parent mentions trying “SOS Super”  
- User says it wasn’t working the other day  
- Now apparently working at 62%  

## Next steps  
- Talk to mom later about missing money  
- Child to take £5 only on outing
